sections.heading: S
sections.section_value: The patient reports persistent right-sided back pain lasting approximately one week, with some radiation to the right lower quadrant. He also reports seeing blood in his urine. He describes the pain as constant. He reports nausea but denies vomiting or fever. He states his father had kidney stones and he believes he has passed a couple in the past. He has never had a kidney stone before.

sections.heading: O
sections.section_value: Blood pressure is within normal limits. Heart rate is 78 beats per minute. Temperature is 98.6°F. Abdominal exam reveals mild tenderness to palpation in the right lower quadrant with no rebound tenderness or guarding. A stone measuring 5 cm is noted in the proximal right ureter on CT imaging. There is no evidence of hydronephrosis.

sections.heading: A
sections.section_value: The patient has a confirmed kidney stone in the right ureter. The pain and hematuria are consistent with this diagnosis. No other acute findings are present.

sections.heading: P
sections.section_value: Rescribe ibuprofen 800 mg orally every 6 hours as needed for pain. Order a BMP, urinalysis, and urine culture. Provide a strainer for urine collection. Schedule follow-up in one to two weeks to assess stone passage and symptom resolution. If infection is present, consider antibiotics. Discuss lithotripsy as a potential further treatment option if stone passage is unsuccessful.
